details.calculation: ($50,753,120 / 1,413,301 total beneficiaries) = $35.91 per outcome. | A $1,000 donation achieves ≈ 27.8 outcomes.

details.elaboration: Quote: 'Rudrapur celebrates successful humane street dog population management, with 95% dog sterilization rate.'
details.formula: Highest level of evidence cited in impact claims

details.calculation: A quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
Focus on high-neglectedness areas (Farmed Animals: 67%, Wild Animals: 2%, Companion Animals: 31%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

metrics.context_qualifier: Policy changes in 8 states reiterated ban on gestation/farrowing crates, potentially impacting over 950,000 pigs.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Pigs would likely continue to be confined in gestation and farrowing crates.

metrics.evidence_quote: Animal husbandry departments of 8 STATE GOVERNMENT issues orders reiterating the PROHIBITION ON GESTATION AND FARROWING CRATES, potentially IMPACTING OVER 950,000 PIGS across these states.
metrics.metric_name: Gestation and Farrowing Crate Ban Impact

metrics.context_qualifier: 1.3 million eggs from caged sources replaced with cage-free sources by 8 hotels.

counterfactual_baseline.description: These eggs would have continued to be sourced from caged hens.

metrics.evidence_quote: We worked with two cage-free producers to get them certified and eight hotels to help them replace 1.3 million eggs from caged sources with cage-free sources.
metrics.source_citation: Humane World for Animals India website

metrics.context_qualifier: Cumulative number of dogs sterilized and vaccinated through the program since 2013.

counterfactual_baseline.description: These dogs would have contributed to uncontrolled population growth and potential suffering.

metrics.evidence_quote: 400,000+ dogs have been sterilized and vaccinated through our program since 2013.
metrics.source_citation: Humane World for Animals India website

metrics.context_qualifier: Cumulative number of animals saved by the India-based disaster response team since 2018.

counterfactual_baseline.description: These animals would have likely perished or suffered without intervention.

metrics.evidence_quote: Our India-based disaster response team has saved more than 250,000 animals' lives since 2018.
metrics.source_citation: Humane World for Animals India website

metrics.context_qualifier: 27,000 Olive Ridley Turtle hatchlings released after nests were relocated and incubated.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Vulnerable nests would have likely been destroyed, leading to fewer hatchlings surviving.

metrics.evidence_quote: Around 27,000 Olive Ridley Turtle hatchlings were released back into the ocean as part of our Olive Ridley Conservation program

metrics.context_qualifier: Relief provided to over 12,000 animals in Kerala, Odisha, and Assam during floods.

counterfactual_baseline.description: These animals would have suffered or perished without intervention.

metrics.evidence_quote: Provided relief to over 12,000 animals in Kerala, Odisha and Assam during floods

metrics.context_qualifier: Over 100,000 animal-based meals replaced with plant-based meals.

counterfactual_baseline.description: These meals would have continued to be animal-based.

metrics.evidence_quote: WE TRAINED OVER 350 CULINARY STUDENTS and replaced over 1,00,000 ANIMAL-BASED MEALS with plant-based meals.

metrics.context_qualifier: 24,268 street dogs and 33 cats sterilized and vaccinated across program locations.

counterfactual_baseline.description: These animals would have contributed to uncontrolled population growth and potential suffering.

metrics.evidence_quote: successfully sterilized and vaccinated 24,268 street dogs and 33 cats across our program locations.
metrics.source_citation: Humane World for Animals India website

metrics.context_qualifier: Lucknow achieved 100,000 dog sterilization surgeries under its Animal Birth Control program.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without the program, this number of sterilizations would not have occurred.

metrics.evidence_quote: Lucknow has achieved a major milestone in humane street dog management by completing one 100,000 dog sterilization surgeries under its Animal Birth Control program, jointly implemented.
metrics.source_citation: Humane World for Animals India website

metrics.context_qualifier: Over 40,000 dogs sterilized in Dehradun since 2017.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without the program, this number of sterilizations would not have occurred.

metrics.evidence_quote: Through spay and neuter and educational campaigns, our programs are changing hearts and minds while providing essential veterinary care and resources the dogs need.
metrics.source_citation: Humane World for Animals India website

metrics.context_qualifier: Rudrapur achieved a 95% dog sterilization rate for humane street dog population management.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Baseline surveys showed zero sterilization among dogs before the program.

metrics.evidence_quote: Rudrapur celebrates successful humane street dog population management, with 95% dog sterilization rate.
metrics.source_citation: Humane World for Animals India website

metrics.context_qualifier: 86% sterilization and vaccination rate achieved for street dogs in Vadodara.

counterfactual_baseline.description: The sterilization rate would have been lower without the program.

metrics.evidence_quote: WE SUCCESSFULLY STERILIZED AND VACCINATED 86% of the street dogs in Vadodara as part of the animal birth control program and handed the program back to the Vadodara Municipal Corporation.

metrics.context_qualifier: Negotiated rules for pesticides and vaccines spared thousands of animals from testing.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Thousands of animals would have suffered and died in needless testing.

metrics.evidence_quote: We helped ban animal testing of cosmetics in India and negotiated rules for pesticides and vaccines that have spared thousands of animals from severe suffering and death in needless testing.
metrics.source_citation: Humane World for Animals India website

metrics.context_qualifier: Zero animals used in cosmetics testing in India due to advocacy efforts.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Animals would still be used for cosmetics testing in India.

metrics.evidence_quote: 0 animals are used in cosmetics testing in India, thanks to years of our advocacy.
metrics.source_citation: Humane World for Animals India website

counterfactual_baseline.description: These facilities would have continued operating without pollution notices.

metrics.evidence_quote: 18 battery-cage facilities were sent notices to for pollution by the Haryana State Pollution Board

counterfactual_baseline.description: These elephants would not have been monitored for human-wildlife conflict mitigation.

metrics.evidence_quote: Collared 8 elephants taking the number of radio - collared elephants in Coorg district to 21

counterfactual_baseline.description: These dogs would have remained in hoarding conditions.

significant_events.event_name: Gestation and Farrowing Crate Ban Advocacy
significant_events.source_quote: Animal husbandry departments of 8 STATE GOVERNMENT issues orders reiterating the PROHIBITION ON GESTATION AND FARROWING CRATES, potentially IMPACTING OVER 950,000 PIGS across these states.
significant_events.summary: Secured reiteration of ban on gestation and farrowing crates in 8 states, impacting over 950,000 pigs.

significant_events.source_quote: We worked with two cage-free producers to get them certified and eight hotels to help them replace 1.3 million eggs from caged sources with cage-free sources.
significant_events.summary: Partnered with 2 producers and 8 hotels to replace 1.3 million caged eggs with cage-free alternatives.

significant_events.source_quote: successfully sterilized and vaccinated 24,268 street dogs and 33 cats across our program locations.
significant_events.summary: Successfully sterilized and vaccinated 24,268 street dogs and 33 cats across program locations this year.

significant_events.source_quote: Provided relief to over 12,000 animals in Kerala, Odisha and Assam during floods
significant_events.summary: Provided relief to over 12,000 animals in Kerala, Odisha, and Assam during floods.

significant_events.source_quote: WE TRAINED OVER 350 CULINARY STUDENTS and replaced over 1,00,000 ANIMAL-BASED MEALS with plant-based meals.
significant_events.summary: Trained over 350 culinary students and replaced over 100,000 animal-based meals with plant-based options.

significant_events.source_quote: THE INDIAN GOVERNMENT PASSED A LAW allowing for non-animal methodologies to be used during drug discovery.
significant_events.summary: Advocated for and achieved a law allowing non-animal methodologies in drug discovery and zero animals in cosmetics testing in India.

significant_events.source_quote: Around 27,000 Olive Ridley Turtle hatchlings were released back into the ocean as part of our Olive Ridley Conservation program
significant_events.summary: Relocated vulnerable nests to hatcheries and released 27,000 hatchlings back into the ocean.

significant_events.source_quote: Government of Karnataka released India's FIRST OPERATION MANUAL ON HUMAN - SNAKE CONFLICT MANAGEMENT for Karnataka, which was CO-AUTHORED BY HSI INDIA.
significant_events.summary: Co-authored India's first operation manual on human-snake conflict management for Karnataka.

significant_events.source_quote: We launched a snakebite app in collaboration with The Liana Trust to provide easily accessible information about snake species found in the local vicinity, as well as lifesaving snakebite first-aid, snakebite prevention tips, and myth-busting around misinformation about snakes that can lead to acts of cruelty.
significant_events.summary: Launched a snakebite app with The Liana Trust for snake species info, first-aid, prevention, and myth-busting.

significant_events.source_quote: Collared 8 elephants taking the number of radio - collared elephants in Coorg district to 21
significant_events.summary: Collared 8 elephants to track and monitor for human-elephant conflict mitigation in Coorg district.

significant_events.event_name: Model District Program in West Bengal
significant_events.source_quote: OUR MODEL DISTRICT PROGRAM in West Bengal saw HSI/India being inducted as member of the State Animal Welfare Board and our staff as members in the Society for Prevention of Cruelty to Animals across districts.
significant_events.summary: HSI/India inducted as member of State Animal Welfare Board and staff as members in SPCA across districts.

significant_events.event_name: Support for Animal Cruelty Legal Cases
significant_events.source_quote: Our efforts to address animal cruelty continued, with support provided to over 12 individuals pursuing legal action for animal cruelty cases.
significant_events.summary: Provided support to over 12 individuals pursuing legal action for animal cruelty cases.

data.original_text: Humane World for Animals is one of the few groups in India striving to protect all animals.
We want to give farmed animals freedom to move, and we advocate for a more plant-based food system.
We want to transition testing and research to animal-free methods and manage street dog populations with compassion.
We aid animals in crisis situations, and we teach people how to peacefully coexist with animals, be it on the streets with dogs or wild animals in nature.
Our India-based disaster response team has saved more than 250,000 animals' lives since 2018.
We assist with preparedness efforts to help communities safely evacuate with their animals before a disaster.
When one strikes, we deploy quickly to provide food, water and veterinary treatment.
We're working to end animal testing for good.
400,000+ dogs have been sterilized and vaccinated through our program since 2013.
250,000+ animals have been saved through our disaster response team's efforts.
0 animals are used in cosmetics testing in India, thanks to years of our advocacy.
Humane World India deployed its animal rescue team to aid relief efforts after devastating landslides and floods hit Wayanad in the summer of 2024.
We provided shelter for affected animals and 200kgs of emergency animal feed to stranded cattle, along with as medical and veterinary supplies, food and water.

We launched a snakebite app in collaboration with The Liana Trust to provide easily accessible information about snake species found in the local vicinity, as well as lifesaving snakebite first-aid, snakebite prevention tips, and myth-busting around misinformation about snakes that can lead to acts of cruelty.
India has close to 70 million street dogs.
We help humanely manage this population by working with veterinarians, local governments and communities on spay/neuter and promoting peaceful coexistence with street dogs.
We helped ban animal testing of cosmetics in India and negotiated rules for pesticides and vaccines that have spared thousands of animals from severe suffering and death in needless testing.
We're working with scientists, businesses and the government to accelerate the development and use of animal-free approaches for testing and research.
Lucknow has achieved a major milestone in humane street dog management by completing one 100,000 dog sterilization surgeries under its Animal Birth Control program, jointly implemented.
Rudrapur celebrates successful humane street dog population management, with 95% dog sterilization rate.

Humane World for Animals India (formerly called Humane Society International/India) is a nonprofit with a proven track record of addressing the root causes of animal cruelty and suffering to create permanent change.

We are doing this through mitigating human-animal conflicts, building disaster response and resilience, capacity-building of enforcement officials, safeguarding street animals, protecting farmed animals, and influencing public policy to reduce animal suffering used for experimentation.
We protect ALL animals!
Beneficiaries: Children and youths. Environment. People with medical needs. Women and girls.
Through spay and neuter and educational campaigns, our programs are changing hearts and minds while providing essential veterinary care and resources the dogs need.
In the capital city of Dehradun, over 40,000 dogs have been sterilized since 2017, including over 6,000 animals brought in by community members.
We're also fighting to end the cruel dog meat trade in India.
We're teaching rural communities how to avoid conflict with elephants and snakes.

In India, we operate in 10+ States and work across several critical areas such as cruelty to animals, street dog management, relief and rehabilitation for animals during disasters, mitigating human- wildlife conflict, building capacity of law enforcement officers on animal welfare laws and its enforcement, farm-animal Welfare and more.
We replaced 134,000 new meals in 2024-2025.
We worked with two cage-free producers to get them certified and eight hotels to help them replace 1.3 million eggs from caged sources with cage-free sources.
The team addressed more than 9,210 complaints and requests related to street dog welfare, underscoring its responsiveness and community-driven approach.
Additionally, 751 street dogs and three cats were vaccinated with community sponsorship in Dehradun and Lucknow.

Over the past year, we conducted 842 meetings that reached more than 5,212 community members, focusing on topics such as managing human-dog conflicts, proper waste disposal and the establishment of feeding and ...
We reached 600,000 people via community radio in Wayanad on May 2024, with our message translated into tribal languages, increasing disaster preparedness awareness for animals.
We were commissioned by the Kerala State Disaster Management Authority to study animal vulnerability, a foundational step for data-driven disaster planning.
successfully sterilized and vaccinated 24,268 street dogs and 33 cats across our program locations.
Our program achieved significant sterilization coverage in key locations Lucknow (84.3%), Dehradun (85%), Mussoorie (92%) and Nainital (99%).
With the successful completion of our goals in Nainital, the program there has now been formally concluded.
The dog population remained stable, evidenced by a low proportion of lactating females (2.1%) and puppies (1.8%).
Baseline surveys in Rudrapur (2024) and Kashipur (2025) showed zero sterilization among dogs before the launch of the Animal Birth Control (ABC) programme in those areas.
Additionally, we collaborated with the Dehradun District Panchayat to conduct a joint manual dog count in selected rural villages, providing a comprehensive report as requested.
Our efforts to address animal cruelty continued, with support provided to over 12 individuals pursuing legal action for animal cruelty cases.

aliases: Humane World for Animals India Foundation
